title: Nursery Rhyme. XXV. Historical
content:
[A song on King William the Third.]

        As I walk'd by myself,
        And talked to myself,
            Myself said unto me,
        Look to thyself,
        Take care of thyself,
            For nobody cares for thee.

        I answer'd myself,
        And said to myself
            In the self-same repartee,
        Look to thyself,
        Or not look to thyself,
            The self-same thing will be.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Unknown delivers a powerful performance in "Nursery Rhyme. XXV. Historical"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
